summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with a lighthearted tone before turning to the central theme of finishing the spiritual race of life strong. He shared his personal goal to be a good finisher in faith, family, and ministry, contrasting it with the common pattern of starting strong but finishing poorly. Using the metaphor of a race from Scripture, he challenged the congregation to examine what kind of race they are currently running—whether aimless, on empty, or distracted. The core of the message provided three practical keys for running the race to win: maintaining spiritual focus, creating healthy life balance, and managing personal energy by casting burdens on God. Pastor Travis emphasized that distractions, like bitterness and an unhealthy focus on the past, are dream-killers that must be overcome by fixing our eyes on Christ and His purpose. He concluded with an invitation for anyone weary or burdened to find rest and salvation in Jesus, highlighting God's unconditional love and the strength found in being yoked with Him.
mainPoints: To finish strong, you must keep your spiritual focus, fixing your eyes on God's purpose and not allowing distractions like bitterness to derail you.
To finish strong, you must keep your life in balance by being intentional with your schedule, creating boundaries, and observing rest as God commanded.
To finish strong, you must manage your energy by casting your burdens of sin, conflict, and weariness onto the Lord to avoid burnout and find rest.

versesMentioned.text: However, I consider my life worth nothing to me; my only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me—the task of testifying to the good news of God’s grace.

I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith. Now there is in store for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day—and not only to me, but also to all who have longed for his appearing.
Let your eyes look straight ahead; fix your gaze directly before you.

Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize. Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever. Therefore I do not run like someone running aimlessly; I do not fight like a boxer beating the air.
See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God and that no bitter root grows up to cause trouble and defile many.
The seed falling among the thorns refers to someone who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, making it unfruitful.

Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me. Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.

There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens: a time to be born and a time to die, a time to plant and a time to uproot, a time to kill and a time to heal, a time to tear down and a time to build, a time to weep and a time to laugh, a time to mourn and a time to dance.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us.
Cast your cares on the LORD and he will sustain you; he will never let the righteous be shaken.

Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.

Monday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - '...Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on...'
Monday.action: Identify one regret or hurt from your past. Write it down, pray for God's healing, and then physically destroy the paper as an act of release.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to forget what is behind. I release [name the hurt/regret] to You today and choose to strain toward the future You have for me.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:25 (NIV) - 'Let your eyes look straight ahead; fix your gaze directly before you.'
Tuesday.action: Identify your biggest distraction today (phone, worry, a relationship). Intentionally set it aside for one hour to pray and read Scripture.
Tuesday.prayer: God, my future follows my focus. Help me fix my eyes on You and Your purpose for me today, not on the distractions around me.

Wednesday.scripture: Hebrews 12:15 (NIV) - 'See to it that... no bitter root grows up to cause trouble...'
Wednesday.action: Make a list of 10 things you are genuinely grateful for. Read it aloud and thank God for each one.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank You for Your goodness to me. I choose gratitude over bitterness. Uproot any bitter root in my heart and replace it with thankfulness.

Thursday.scripture: Ecclesiastes 3:1 (NIV) - 'There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens.'
Thursday.action: Review your calendar for the next week. Prayerfully cancel or reschedule one non-essential activity to create space for rest or family.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, teach me the rhythm of Your seasons. Help me create a balanced life that honors You with both work and rest.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 55:22 (NIV) - 'Cast your cares on the LORD and he will sustain you...'
Friday.action: Write down your heaviest burden or worry on a card. In prayer, physically lift it up and verbally give it to God. Keep the card as a reminder to leave it with Him.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I am weary and burdened. I cast my care about [name the burden] onto You right now. I trust You to sustain me. Thank You for Your rest.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 11:28-29 (NIV) - 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'
Saturday.action: Intentionally disconnect from work emails and social media for several hours. Do something that refreshes your soul—take a walk, listen to worship music, or enjoy a hobby.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I come to You today for rest. As I step back from my work, teach me what it means to find true soul-rest in Your presence.
